sections.paragraph:
λείπω fut. 3 sg. λείψει ApcEsdr; 2 aor. ἔλιπον, subj. 3 sg. λίπῃ Tit 3:13 v.l.; pf. pass. ptc. λελειμμένος 2 Macc 4:45 (Hom.+, ‘leave [behind]’ and other senses).

① to leave behind, trans., mid. and pass.

ⓐ fall short, be inferior, lack (Hdt. 7, 8, 1; Diod S 17, 46, 1; Jos., Bell. 3, 482, Ant. 14, 474; Just., A I, 67, 1 and 6) ἔν τινι in someth. (Sb 620, 6 [97/96 b.c.] of a temple λείπεσθαι ἐν τῷ μὴ εἶναι ἄσυλον; SIG 618, 15; 800, 29; PGM 4, 2347) ἐν μηδενί fall short in no respect Js 1:4.

ⓑ be/do without, lack, be in need or want (of) w. gen. (B-D-F §180, 4; cp. Soph., El. 474; Ps.-Pla., Axioch. 366d; later, e.g. Libanius, Progym.: Confirm. 1, 1 vol. VIII p. 138f. τῆς ἐνθέου μανίας) σοφίας be deficient in wisdom Js 1:5. τῆς ἐφημέρου τροφῆς be in need of daily food 2:15. θεοῦ lack God ITr 5:2b. μηδενός lack nothing IPol 2:2.

② to be deficient in someth. that ought to be present for whatever reason, lack, intr., act. (λείπει τί τινι: Polyb. 10, 18, 8; Epict. 2, 14, 19; Jos., Ant. 12, 36; λείπει τίς τινι ApcEsdr 6:18 p. 31, 27 Tdf.) σοὶ πολλὰ λείπει you fall far short Hv 3, 1, 9. πολλὰ ἡμῖν λείπει we lack much ITr 5:2a. ἔτι ἕν σοι λείπει there is one thing that you still lack Lk 18:22 (cp. Jos., Bell. 4, 257 τοῖς τολμήμασιν ἓν μόνον λείπει; TestAbr A 14 p. 93, 26f [Stone p. 34] τί ἔτι λείπεται τῇ ψυχῇ εἰς τὸ σώζεσθαι;) ἵνα μηδὲν αὐτοῖς λείπῃ that they may lack nothing Tit 3:13. τὰ λείποντα (Just., A I, 52, 2; τὰ λ. τοῦ ψαλμοῦ Just., D 106, 1; Lucian, Syr. Dea 26) what is lacking, the defects 1:5. W. inf. foll. (cp. B-D-F §393, 4) λείπει τῷ πύργῳ ἔτι μικρὸν οἰκοδομηθῆναι the tower still lacks a little of being finished, is still not quite finished Hs 9, 9, 4.—B. 839. DELG. M-M. Spicq.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

aor. aor. = aorist

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

trans. trans. = transitive

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

gen. gen. = genitive

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Libanius Libanius , IV a.d.—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

intr. intr. = intransitive

act. act. = active

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Hv Hv = Visions

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 590
